nameMeanings.name: Neumann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Gunter, Helmut, Erwin, Heinz, Dieter, Guenter, Bernd, Ernst, Fritz.

German, Danish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a newcomer to a place, from Middle High German niuwe (German neu) ‘new’ + man (German Mann) ‘man’. This surname is also found in Czechia, Poland, Sweden, France (Alsace and Lorraine), and some other European countries.

variants.name: Neiman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Moshe, Pincus, Chaim, Hershel, Sender, Avrohom, Baruch, Bronia, Elchonon, Eliyohu, Gershon, Hyman.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Neumann   ‘newcomer’. This form of the surname is also found in Sweden.
Americanized form of German   Neimann   ‘newcomer’.

variants.name: Neeman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Aron, Giora, Menashe, Amnon, Limor, Moshe, Shaul.
Jewish: occupational name from a Hebrew term meaning ‘treasurer ofthe community’. In some cases this may represent a Hebraized (Israeli)form of   Neumann  .

variants.name: Naiman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Yosef, Ari, Chaya, Elimelech, Emanuel, Hyman, Isadore, Mayer, Shlomo, Yitzchok.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): Yiddish equivalent of German   Neumann   ‘newcomer’. Compare   Newman  .

variants.name: Naumann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Otto, Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Horst, Joerg, Arno, Bernd, Bernhardt, Berthold, Egon.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Neumann  .

variants.name: Newman
meanings: English (southern): nickname from Middle English newe ‘new’ (i.e. newly arrived or newly appointed) + man ‘man’ (Old English nīwe + mann).

Americanized form (translation into English) of surnames meaning ‘new man, newcomer’, for example German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Neumann , Swedish Nyman , Polish Nowak , Czech or Slovak Novák, Slovenian, Croatian, or Serbian Novak .

variants.name: Newmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Heinz, Markus.
Americanized form of German   Neumann  .

variants.name: Neimann
meanings: German: variant of   Neumann   ‘newcomer’.

variants.name: Nejman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jadwiga, Jerzy, Zygmunt.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): of German origin, from the Polish adaptation of   Neumann  .

variants.name: Neuman
meanings: Americanized form of German or Danish   Neumann  , and a variant of the same Jewish (Ashkenazic) surname. This form of the surname is also found in Czechia, Poland, Sweden, the Netherlands, and some other European countries.

variants.name: Nayman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Esfir, Shmuel, Yakov.  Russian Lev, Mikhail, Oleg, Yelena.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from Yiddish nayman ‘new man’(see Neumann ).

nameMeanings.name: Adam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical. See Adam .

th.–: ถึงแก่กรรม
th.–Female: ถึงแก่กรรม
th.–Male: ถึงแก่กรรม
